mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.10%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, mechanically supporting elevated equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as policy rate stays near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.10 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX produces negative average 60-day returns during sustained near-zero FEDFUNDS periods (p < 0.05 vs baseline).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market repair after the COVID shock, boosting consumer confidence and forward earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.4 as of t-1mo, down sharply from pandemic peak near 14.7
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE drops >0.5pp MoM, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) across at least two rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates simultaneous recovery of consumer spending and residential investment, a combination that historically signals a broadening demand rebound stronger than either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 1% AND HOUST MoM growth > 5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1496k as of t-2mo indicating strong housing rebound
fact:RSAFS=535982M as of t-2mo indicating retail recovery
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration episodes are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-COVID-shock recovery regime, month-over-month gains in INDPRO reflect reopening of manufacturing capacity and supply-chain normalization, which supports corporate earnings recovery and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, but this relationship is regime-specific to the recovery phase and not generalizable to late-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.36 as of t-1mo, rebounding from pandemic trough
regime_hint: post-shock industrial recovery from COVID-19 contraction
failure_condition: During the identified post-shock recovery window, positive INDPRO MoM changes fail to predict positive 60-day forward SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.03, or the sign of the relationship flips once INDPRO surpasses its pre-pandemic peak.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market healing and directly informs near-term consumer income and spending capacity, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in payroll data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=140914k as of t-1mo, rising from pandemic trough
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 1.5%, markets begin pricing in the risk of earlier-than-expected policy normalization by the Federal Reserve, raising discount rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
regime_hint: early-stage inflation re-acceleration under near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of historical instances where CPI YoY crosses above 1.5% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, or Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero (|IC| < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 combined with accelerating RSAFS growth signals a self-reinforcing aggregate demand recovery in which consumption growth confirms and amplifies the GDP rebound signal, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; the interaction is necessary because GDP alone is low-frequency and lagged while RSAFS confirms the recovery is broad and ongoing.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth turns positive AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=17282.188B as of t-5mo reflecting Q3 rebound expectations
fact:RSAFS=535982M as of t-2mo showing continued retail strength
failure_condition: Joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS signals fail to produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) in more than one out of two historical recovery episodes, or interaction term underperforms single-proxy GDPC1 model.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery regime where policy rates are held near zero, an elevated but declining UNRATE level (still above 8%) is interpreted by markets as slack that guarantees continued monetary accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; this relationship reverses once UNRATE falls toward pre-recession lows because slack no longer supports the accommodation narrative.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.4 while FEDFUNDS=0.10 as of snapshot, indicating high slack with accommodative policy
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with near-zero policy rate and elevated labor slack
failure_condition: As UNRATE falls below 6% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, positive 20-day SPX returns associated with high-UNRATE/low-FEDFUNDS combination fail to persist, or the sign of the relationship flips without a corresponding shift in FEDFUNDS regime, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.
